name: Boeing
cat: Aerospace & Defense / Commercial and Military Aircraft

political.s: <cite index="2-1">Boeing has donated about $2.5 million to federal political groups, primarily to the Republican National Committee and Democratic National Committee</cite>, indicating mixed political alignment. <cite index="4-1">Boeing spent $11,340,000 lobbying in 2025</cite>, demonstrating significant investment in federal influence across both parties.

charity.s: <cite index="11-1">Donations from the Boeing Company Charitable Trust typically range from $50,000 to $1.6 million</cite>. <cite index="11-10">Boeing's community impact programs support STEM education, community development, disaster relief and veterans' causes</cite>, with <cite index="12-9">a dollar-for-dollar match for eligible charitable donations</cite>.
charity.focus: STEM education, disaster relief, veterans, community development

environment.s: <cite index="27-1">For the fourth consecutive year in 2023, Boeing achieved net-zero carbon emissions across Scope 1, Scope 2, and parts of Scope 3 by combining renewable energy investments, conservation efforts, and verified carbon offsets</cite>. <cite index="22-9">Boeing's newest airplanes are 20-30% more efficient than the in-service airplanes they replace</cite>.

labor.s: <cite index="31-1">Boeing has been hit with 32 whistleblower complaints since 2020, according to a report of the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)</cite>. <cite index="31-16,31-17,31-18">The FAA's Organization Designation Authorization Expert Review Panel reviewed Boeing's safety management processes and found numerous deficiencies in its safety culture, criticizing it for a lack of awareness of safety-related metrics at all levels</cite>. <cite index="34-1">Boeing has accumulated penalty totals of $4,912,024,372 since 2000 across 128 records</cite>.

dei.s: <cite index="42-2">Boeing has disbanded its Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion department, combining its DEI efforts with a broader HR team</cite>. <cite index="45-4,45-5">The company suspended funding for its diversity councils and business resource group programs, citing a 'dynamically shifting environment in the United States'</cite>.

animals.s: Not applicable to aerospace manufacturing company.

guns.s: Boeing manufactures military aircraft, defense systems, and weapons platforms for government and military customers, constituting a core business segment.

privacy.s: Limited public information available on Boeing's data privacy practices and policies.

execPay.s: <cite index="52-1">CEO Kelly Ortberg's total compensation of $23.58 million compared to median employee compensation of $141,933 results in a Boeing CEO pay ratio of 166 to 1</cite>. This significantly exceeds typical S&P 500 ratios and reflects substantial executive compensation disparity.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
